title: Udaipur 2-Day Romantic Getaway
metaTitle: Udaipur 2-Day Romantic Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to Udaipur, India: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including City Palace, Udaipur and Jag Mandir. Prices from $1311 per person (estimate).
highlights: Overnight stay at the Taj Lake Palace on Lake Pichola
Sunset boat ride to Jag Mandir
overview: Spend 2 days discovering Udaipur, India — Milder than the Rajasthan desert cities thanks to its lake-and-hills setting: hot but not extreme summers (highs 36-38°C in May), a good monsoon July-September that fills the lakes, and pleasant winters (highs 25-27°C, cool nights around 8-10°C). September to March, when lake levels are good and highs sit around 24-32°C; avoid May-June heat, though the lakes can look dramatic post-monsoon in September. This itinerary blends City Palace, Udaipur, Jag Mandir, Ghats of Lake Pichola with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including City Palace, Udaipur, Jag Mandir, Ghats of Lake Pichola
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Udaipur
itinerary.description: Land at Maharana Pratap Airport (UDR) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to City Palace, Udaipur — Largest palace complex in Rajasthan overlooking Lake Pichola. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Udaipur Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to Jag Mandir — Island palace reached by boat across Lake Pichola. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to UDR for your onward flight.

travelTips: Book a sunset boat ride on Lake Pichola in advance during peak season (Oct-Mar) — slots fill fast.
Watch the nightly Dharohar folk dance show at Bagore Ki Haveli for an authentic Mewar culture experience.
Rooftop restaurants around Jagdish Temple offer some of the best value City Palace views.
Combine Kumbhalgarh Fort and Ranakpur Jain Temples into one full-day trip from Udaipur.
September, right after monsoon, offers full lakes and dramatic views with far fewer tourists than winter.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Light layers — warm days but cool lake-breeze evenings, especially Nov-Feb
Modest clothing for temples and the City Palace
Comfortable shoes for palace courtyards and ghat steps
A light jacket for winter mornings and Sajjangarh sunset visits
thingsToDo: Paragliding over the Aravalli hills near Udaipur
Trekking to Sajjangarh through the wildlife sanctuary
Zip-lining and rappelling at nearby adventure parks
Cycling tours through rural villages around Udaipur
Fateh Sagar Lake boating and Nehru Garden island
Shilpgram crafts village and puppet shows
thingsToAvoid: Miniature-painting shops that push hard-sell "artist studio" tours with inflated prices.
Unlicensed boat operators away from the official Lake Pichola jetty.
Overpaying for scooter rentals without checking the vehicle for existing damage first.
Visiting Sajjangarh (Monsoon Palace) in heavy monsoon fog when the view is obscured.
Wandering isolated ghats alone after dark.
topRestaurants: Upre by 1559 AD — Modern Indian, rooftop ($$$)
Jagat Niwas Palace Rooftop — North Indian ($$)
Sheesh Mahal (Fateh Prakash Palace) — Royal Rajasthani, fine dining ($$$$)
nightlife: Dharohar folk dance show at Bagore Ki Haveli
Sunset boat cruise on Lake Pichola

festivalCalendar.name: Shilpgram Utsav
festivalCalendar.timing: Mid-December, rural arts and crafts fair

localEtiquette: Dress modestly at City Palace and temples — cover shoulders and knees.
Ask before photographing locals, particularly at ghats where daily rituals and washing take place.
Remove shoes before entering the Jagdish Temple and other shrines.
Bargain politely in Hathi Pol and Bara Bazaar, but expect fixed prices at government emporiums.
Lake Pichola and Fateh Sagar are considered sacred by many locals — avoid littering or disruptive behavior on boat tours.
localTransportGuide: Udaipur's old city is compact and walkable around the lakes; auto-rickshaws and Ola/Uber cover longer hops like Sajjangarh or the airport. Renting a scooter is popular for exploring Fateh Sagar and nearby villages independently, though traffic can be chaotic near the Clock Tower area.
bestTimeToVisit: September to March, when lake levels are good and highs sit around 24-32°C; avoid May-June heat, though the lakes can look dramatic post-monsoon in September.
weather: Milder than the Rajasthan desert cities thanks to its lake-and-hills setting: hot but not extreme summers (highs 36-38°C in May), a good monsoon July-September that fills the lakes, and pleasant winters (highs 25-27°C, cool nights around 8-10°C).
visaInformation: Most foreign nationals require an Indian e-Visa arranged online before arrival. Indian citizens travel visa-free, and OCI/PIO cardholders enter visa-free with a linked foreign passport — relevant for the many NRI couples who choose Udaipur for destination weddings.
averageFlightTime: ~9.4h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Udaipur?
faqs.answer: Most foreign nationals require an Indian e-Visa arranged online before arrival. Indian citizens travel visa-free, and OCI/PIO cardholders enter visa-free with a linked foreign passport — relevant for the many NRI couples who choose Udaipur for destination weddings.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Udaipur?
faqs.answer: September to March, when lake levels are good and highs sit around 24-32°C; avoid May-June heat, though the lakes can look dramatic post-monsoon in September. Milder than the Rajasthan desert cities thanks to its lake-and-hills setting: hot but not extreme summers (highs 36-38°C in May), a good monsoon July-September that fills the lakes, and pleasant winters (highs 25-27°C, cool nights around 8-10°C).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Udaipur safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Udaipur is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Udaipur?
faqs.answer: The local currency is INR. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Udaipur is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Sunset boat ride to Jag Mandir experience.

reviews.author: Grace Sharma
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Saheliyon Ki Bari and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Marco Ibrahim
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Ghats of Lake Pichola and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Olivia Smith
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: Our trip to Udaipur was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Sajjangarh (Monsoon Palace) was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Anna Botha
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: Udaipur is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Jagdish Temple. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Liam Cohen
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Our trip to Udaipur was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Bagore Ki Haveli was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Mohammed Khan
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Jagdish Temple alone was worth the trip, but combined with Clock Tower (Ghanta Ghar) this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Chen Silva
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Clock Tower (Ghanta Ghar) in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.
